evidence: Docstring: 'If f(z) = ∑ aₖzⁿₖ is an entire function ... is it true that f assumes every value infinitely often?'
The hypothesis is HasFabryGaps n (nₖ/k → ∞), the gap condition of the Fabry gap theorem.
Docstring: entire function with Fejér gaps (∑ 1/nₖ < ∞) assumes every value infinitely often, proved in [Bi28].
The statement concerns gap (lacunary) power series f(z) = ∑ aₖ zⁿₖ with sparse exponents.
References cite Biró, 'On a problem of Turán concerning sums of powers of complex numbers' and 'Turán's pure power sum problem'; the statement bounds max_k |∑ zᵢᵏ|.
The statement max(|A+A|,|AA|) ≫ |A|^(2-ε) is the Erdős–Szemerédi sum-product conjecture (erdosproblems.com tags it 'sum-product problem').
The statement bounds the cardinality of the sumset A+A (and product set AA) of a finite set of integers.
The statement is about random Rademacher multiplicative functions f with f(ab)=f(a)f(b) for coprime a,b.
Asks for an almost-sure limsup of ∑_{m≤N} f(m) / sqrt(N log log N), a law-of-the-iterated-logarithm normalization.
Docstring: monochromatic finite subset sums for a 2-colouring of N, 'Proved by Hindman [Hi74] (for any number of colours)' — this is Hindman's finite sums theorem.
Docstring defines the triangle Ramsey–Turán number RT₃(n, K₅, m) and asks whether δ₃(5) = 0, citing the Turán-Ramsey theorems of [EHSSS94].
Asks whether a dense K₅-free graph must contain a set of ≫ n vertices spanning no triangle (a triangle-free vertex set).
f_r(N) is the largest subset of {1,...,N} with no r-element subset having the same pairwise greatest common divisor.
Docstring: Abbott pointed out the ordinary sunflower conjecture does not suffice; this is the stronger sunflower-type auxiliary conjecture with Ω(n)=k.
Docstring: must there be distinct a,b,c in A with [a,b]=[b,c]=[a,c], 'where [·,·] denotes the least common multiple'.
